title: Aletes acaulis
intro: Aletes acaulis, also known as western yarrow, is a perennial herb in the carrot family native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Aletes acaulis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aletes acaulis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat colds, sore throats, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Aletes acaulis is classified within the plant family Apiaceae. This plant family is commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to the mountainous regions of Europe, specifically the Pyrenees. Aletes acaulis is closely related to other genera within the Apiaceae family, such as Ferula and Ligusticum. Further classification of Aletes acaulis may be found within the tribe Apioideae.
